reasoning: The Federal Reserve has made 0 rate cuts so far in 2025, maintaining the federal funds target range at 4.25%-4.50% through the July meeting. Only three meetings remain in 2025 (September, October, and December). For this market to resolve YES, the Fed must execute exactly one 25 bps cut (or a cut up to 24 bps) with no other cuts. Market pricing currently puts at least one cut in September at >90% probability (with exactly a 25 bps cut strongly favored over a 50 bps cut). However, market projections and major analysts (Goldman Sachs, Citi, etc.) forecast multiple rate cuts, expecting 2-4 total cuts before the end of the year. According to late-August reporting, a second cut is "about fully priced in" by December. Given the likelihood of a second cut, the probability of exactly 1 rate cut occurring is driven down to the scenario where economic data rebounds post-September, forcing the Fed to pause its easing cycle in Q4. This scenario carries approximately an 18% probability.
